description_long: This hammer features a cylindrical head with a uniform diameter and flat striking faces on both ends, suggesting a mallet or sledgehammer style. The handle is straight and round, covered in a colorful, segmented pattern with numbers and symbols, possibly for decorative or educational purposes. The head is also decorated with similar colorful patterns and markings, giving the tool a playful and distinctive appearance. The hammer appears to be made primarily of wood or a lightweight composite, as indicated by the smooth, painted surface and the lack of visible metal parts. There are no visible signs of wear, damage, or branding on the object. The overall design is simple, with no additional attachments or ergonomic enhancements.
description: A colorful, cylindrical-headed hammer with a straight, patterned handle and flat striking faces, featuring decorative numbers and symbols.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full cylindrical head with both flat faces and the straight, patterned handle.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the narrow profile of the head and the full length of the handle with its segmented color pattern.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the opposite flat face of the head and the handle's colorful segments.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the head's narrow width and the continuation of the handle's decorative pattern.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a round cross-section, covered in a smooth, painted, segmented pattern featuring numbers and symbols.
attribute_head_type: Cylindrical mallet-style head with flat striking faces on both ends, decorated with colorful patterns and markings.
